query: AdaptyvBio protein binder competition results history success rates 2024 2025 2026
answer:
Now I have comprehensive data to compile a thorough report. Let me produce the structured output.

## AdaptyvBio Protein Binder Competition: Complete Results History (2024–2026)

### Overview

Adaptyv Bio (based in Switzerland) has run a series of open, crowdsourced protein binder design competitions since mid-2024, rapidly scaling in scope and participation. Their platform, Proteinbase, hosts all experimental data open-source. As of April 2026, they have experimentally validated over 2,000 protein designs across multiple targets, yielding hundreds of confirmed binders. A fourth competition (RBX1, co-organized with GEM for ICLR 2026) is currently active.

---

### Competition 1: EGFR Binder Design – Round 1 (Summer 2024)

- **Target**: EGFR (Epidermal Growth Factor Receptor), PDB: 1IVO
- **Timeline**: Submissions open Jul 18, 2024 → Close Aug 18, 2024 → Results released Oct 18, 2024
- **Participants**: ~50+ designers
- **Submissions**: ~726 total designs submitted; 201 selected for experimental validation (100 by best pAE_interaction, ~100 by "rule of cool")
- **Expression rate**: 73–76% (146/201); some sources cite 92% in the Proteinbase collection
- **Binding hit rate**: **2.5%** (5 strong binders out of 201 tested), plus 2 weak binders = 7 total binders
- **Best KD**: 6.6 nM (an scFv)
- **KD range**: 3e-8 M to 2.3e-5 M
- **Dominant methods**: RFdiffusion for backbone generation, ProteinMPNN for sequence design
- **Winning approach**: AlphaFold2 hallucination + SolubleMPNN inverse-folding
- **Key finding**: 2.5% hit rate was already considerably higher than previously reported EGFR campaigns (0.01% with Rosetta, Cao et al. 2022)

---

### Competition 2: EGFR Binder Design – Round 2 (Fall 2024)

- **Target**: EGFR (same target, with neutralization assay added)
- **Timeline**: Submissions open Oct 4, 2024 → Close Nov 4, 2024 → Results released Dec 8–24, 2024
- **Participants**: ~130 designers (up from ~50)
- **Submissions**: ~1,000+ designs; 400 selected for validation (doubled from Round 1)
- **Expression rate**: **95–96%** (378/400) — up from 76%
- **Binding hit rate**: **13–14%** (53/400) — a **5× improvement** over Round 1
- **Best KD**: 1.21 nM (Cradle's optimized Cetuximab scFv variant — 8× better than Merck's commercial Cetuximab)
- **26 single-digit nM binders** in this round
- **30 out of 130 designers** produced at least one successful binder

**Hit rates by design category:**
| Category | Hit Rate | Expression Rate |
|---|---|---|
| Diversified binders | 28% | 92% |
| Optimized binders | 18% | — |
| De novo (BindCraft/hallucination) | 9% (6/65) | 100% |
| De novo (RFdiffusion/diffusion) | ~1 binder | — |
| Top 100 selected designs | 24% | — |
| Pseudo-random (diversity) | 10% | — |

**Notable performers:**
- **Cradle** (1st place): Optimized Cetuximab scFv with 10 mutations, achieved 1.21 nM KD
- **Chris Xu / Tihumab** (2nd & 3rd): Nanobodies via antibody humanization
- **Aurelia Bustos** (80% hit rate): Modified TGFα with diffusion models; 8/10 designs bound
- **Lennart Nickel**: Fully de novo binders via BindCraft

**Community paper**: Published on bioRxiv (April 2025): "Crowdsourced Protein Design: Lessons From the Adaptyv EGFR Binder Competition" (doi: 10.1101/2025.04.17.648362v2). Also introduced **BenchBB** (Bench-tested Binder Benchmark).

---

### Competition 3: Nipah Virus Glycoprotein G Binder (Fall 2025–Winter 2026)

- **Target**: Nipah Virus Glycoprotein G (NiV-G), PDB: 2VSM — a challenging tetrameric target with 40–75% mortality and no approved treatments
- **Timeline**: Submissions open Oct 30, 2025 → Close Dec 1, 2025 → Validation starts Dec 11, 2025 → Results released Jan 21, 2026
- **Participants**: **680+** designers (massive growth from 130)
- **Submissions**: **10,000+** designs (5× more than both previous competitions combined)
- **Selected for validation**: **1,200** designs (600 by Boltz-2 ipSAE score, 400 by expert panel, 200 by community vote)
- **Designs experimentally characterized**: **1,030** (of 1,200; remaining 174 had characterization difficulties)
- **Expression rate**: **86%** (881/1,026)
- **Binding hit rate**: **~9.6%** (99 binders out of 1,026 tested) — lower than EGFR Round 2 but against a much harder target with little prior work
- **High-affinity binders**: **26 single-digit nM or better**, with best at **picomolar** affinity
- **Total binders listed on Proteinbase**: 103 (including some from additional data)

**Hit rates by design tool (from Deniz Kavi's analysis):**
| Tool | # Entries | Hit Rate |
|---|---|---|
| Escalante's Mosaic | 9 | **88.8%** |
| RFdiffusion | 42 | **21.4%** |
| ProteinMPNN | 29 | **20.7%** |
| BindCraft | 88 | **5.68%** |
| BoltzGen | 163 | **1.23%** |

**Top winners:**
- 🥇 **Miles McGibbon** — General ranking #1 (KD: 3.7e-10 M / 370 pM)
- 🥈 **Liviu Copoiu** — General #2 (KD: 4.5e-10 M / 450 pM)
- 🥉 **Andrew Huang** — General #3 (KD: 7.2e-10 M / 720 pM)
- De novo #1: **Nick Boyd** (Escalante) — KD: 1.4e-9 M; **9 out of 10 designs bound (90% hit rate)**

**Notable findings:**
- Boltz-2 ipSAE as a filter: Top 0.5% by min_ipsae achieved 83.3% binders (5/6); top 1% achieved 63.6% (7/11)
- RFdiffusion resolved as having the highest model-level hit rate on Manifold prediction markets
- BindCraft2 (pre-release) and Latent-X both achieved ~30% hit rates
- Nipah target was significantly harder than EGFR (tetrameric, little prior binder work)

---

### Competition 4: RBX1 Binder Design (Active, 2026)

- **Target**: RBX1 (RING Box Protein 1 / ROC1) — 108 amino acid E3 ubiquitin ligase component, cancer-relevant
- **Co-organized with**: GEM Workshop at ICLR 2026 (April 27, 2026, Rio de Janeiro)
- **Proteins to be validated**: 300
- **Status**: Active, ends in ~27 days (as of mid-April 2026)
- **Results**: To be announced at ICLR 2026

---

### Cumulative Platform Statistics (as of April 2026, from Proteinbase)

| Target | Proteins Validated | Binders Found |
|---|---|---|
| Nipah Virus Glycoprotein G | 1,030 | 103 |
| EGFR | 826 | 134 |
| PD-L1 | 110 | 40 |
| Human Serum Albumin | 103 | 0 |
| IL-7Ra | 97 | 62 |
| **Total across all targets** | **~2,100+** | **~340+** |

### Trajectory of Key Metrics Across Competitions

| Metric | EGFR R1 (Oct 2024) | EGFR R2 (Dec 2024) | Nipah (Jan 2026) |
|---|---|---|---|
| Designs tested | 201 | 400 | 1,030 |
| Expression rate | 73–76% | 95–96% | 86% |
| Overall hit rate | 2.5% | 13–14% | ~9.6% |
| Best KD | 6.6 nM | 1.21 nM | 370 pM |
| Participants | ~50 | ~130 | 680+ |
| Total submissions | ~726 | ~1,000+ | 10,000+ |

key_facts: Adaptyv Bio has run 3 completed protein binder design competitions (EGFR Round 1, EGFR Round 2, Nipah) and has a 4th (RBX1) currently active as of April 2026
EGFR Round 1 (Oct 2024): 201 designs tested, 2.5% binding hit rate, 73-76% expression rate, ~50 participants, best KD 6.6 nM
EGFR Round 2 (Dec 2024): 400 designs tested, 13-14% binding hit rate (5x improvement over Round 1), 95-96% expression rate, 130 participants, best KD 1.21 nM (Cradle, 8x better than Cetuximab)
Nipah Competition (Jan 2026): 1,030 designs characterized out of 1,200 selected, 9.6% hit rate (99 binders), 86% expression rate, 680+ participants, 10,000+ submissions, best KD 370 pM
Nipah was a significantly harder target than EGFR (tetrameric protein with little prior binder work), explaining the lower hit rate vs EGFR Round 2
In Nipah competition, Escalante's Mosaic tool achieved 88.8% hit rate (n=9), RFdiffusion 21.4% (n=42), BindCraft 5.68% (n=88), BoltzGen 1.23% (n=163)
Nick Boyd (Escalante) won de novo category in Nipah with 9/10 designs binding (90% success rate) using gradient-based optimization directly on Boltz-2 loss without inverse folding or filtering
Participation grew dramatically: ~50 designers (EGFR R1) → 130 (EGFR R2) → 680+ (Nipah)
Submissions grew from ~726 (EGFR R1) to 10,000+ (Nipah) — a 14x increase
Cumulative across all Proteinbase data: ~2,100+ proteins validated, ~340+ binders found across targets including EGFR, Nipah, PD-L1, IL-7Ra, HSA
RBX1 competition (4th) is co-organized with GEM Workshop at ICLR 2026, with 300 proteins to be validated, results expected late April 2026
Community paper published on bioRxiv April 2025: 'Crowdsourced Protein Design: Lessons From the Adaptyv EGFR Binder Competition', also introduced BenchBB benchmark
Boltz-2 ipSAE proved effective as a filter in Nipah: top 0.5% by min_ipsae achieved 83.3% binders
Expression rates have generally improved over time: 73-76% (EGFR R1) → 95-96% (EGFR R2); Nipah was 86% but for a harder target
Best binding affinities improved from 6.6 nM (EGFR R1) to 1.21 nM (EGFR R2) to 370 pM (Nipah)
